# Kubernetes Recipes

> Kubernetes Recipes is a collection of 740+ production-ready Kubernetes guides, tutorials, and troubleshooting recipes by Luca Berton. Covers deployments, networking, security, storage, AI/GPU workloads, Helm, observability, and autoscaling.

- [All Recipes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/): Full recipe index
- [Complete AI-readable index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt): All 740+ recipes with descriptions

## Ai (107 recipes)

- [AI Content Watermarking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-content-watermarking-kubernetes/): Deploy AI-generated content watermarking on Kubernetes. Invisible watermarks, SynthID integration, detection APIs, image and text watermarking pipelines.
- [AI Infrastructure Cost Optimization on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-infrastructure-cost-optimization-kubernetes/): Optimize AI infrastructure costs on Kubernetes. GPU sharing, spot instances, inference batching, model quantization, token economics, and right-sizing GPU workloads.
- [AI Model Storage: hostPath vs PVC for Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/model-storage-hostpath-pvc/): Deploy AI models on Kubernetes using hostPath and PVC storage. Compare performance, security trade-offs, and production patterns for model serving.
- [AI Supercomputing on Kubernetes GPU Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-supercomputing-kubernetes-gpu-clusters/): Build AI supercomputing platforms on Kubernetes. Multi-node GPU training, NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, InfiniBand RDMA, NCCL tuning, Blackwell clusters, and job scheduling.
- [AI-Native Development Platforms on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-native-development-kubernetes/): Build AI-native development platforms on Kubernetes. AI coding agents, automated testing, Copilot infrastructure, dev containers, and AI-driven CI/CD pipelines.
- ... and 102 more ai recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Autoscaling (19 recipes)

- [Advanced Cluster Autoscaler Configuration and Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-cluster-autoscaler-advanced/): Fine-tune the Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler with expanders, priority-based scaling, mixed instance policies, and GPU node pool autoscaling for production clusters.
- [Custom Metrics Autoscaling in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-custom-metrics-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes pods on custom application metrics with Prometheus Adapter. Configure HPA with custom and external metrics beyond CPU and memory.
- [Goldilocks: VPA Recommendations Dashboard](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-goldilocks-vpa-dashboard/): Deploy Goldilocks to visualize VPA recommendations across namespaces. Right-size Kubernetes resource requests and limits with a web dashboard.
- [HPA in Kubernetes: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/horizontal-pod-autoscaler/): HPA in Kubernetes explained with YAML examples. Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for CPU, memory, and custom metrics with scaling policies and VPA comparison.
- [How to Configure Cluster Autoscaler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler/): Automatically scale your Kubernetes cluster nodes based on workload demand. Learn to configure Cluster Autoscaler for AWS, GCP, and Azure.
- ... and 14 more autoscaling recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Configuration (118 recipes)

- [400 Recipes Milestone: What We Built and What's Next](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-recipes-400-pages-milestone/): Kubernetes Recipes reaches 400 articles. Explore new AI/GPU infrastructure, NVIDIA networking, ArgoCD GitOps, OpenShift, and RHACS security recipes.
- [Automate MCP Updates with Drain Script](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-update-automation-script/): Bash script to automate OpenShift MachineConfigPool updates when drains are blocked by PDB violations. Auto-detects blockers, scales down, drains, and restores.
- [ClusterPolicy MOFED Upgrade Strategy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/clusterpolicy-mofed-upgrade/): Configure safe MOFED driver upgrade policies in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy with rolling updates, node draining, and rollback procedures.
- [Configure ClusterPolicy kernelModuleType for GPU Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configure-clusterpolicy-kernel-module-type/): Understand and configure the driver.kernelModuleType field in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy to choose between auto, open, and proprietary kernel.
- [Configure Container Registries via MachineConfig](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-registries-conf/): Set up mirror registries and blocked registries on OpenShift nodes using MachineConfig to control CRI-O image pull on RHCOS.
- ... and 113 more configuration recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Deployments (153 recipes)

- [Air-Gapped Image Import for OpenShift Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/air-gapped-image-import-openshift/): Import container images into disconnected OpenShift clusters. Use podman save/load and internal registries when DNS and TLS block external pulls.
- [Air-Gapped OpenShift with Quay Mirror](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/airgapped-openshift-quay-mirror/): Deploy OpenShift in air-gapped environments with local Quay registry mirror, ImageDigestMirrorSet, and custom CatalogSources.
- [Argo Workflows on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argo-workflows-kubernetes/): deployments
- [ArgoCD App of Apps Pattern](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-pattern/): Implement the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern to manage multiple applications from a parent Application for cluster bootstrapping.
- [ArgoCD App of Apps with Sync Waves](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-sync-waves/): Combine the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern with sync waves to bootstrap entire clusters in dependency order, from CRDs and operators to application workloads.
- ... and 148 more deployments recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Helm (34 recipes)

- [ArgoCD App of Apps with Helm Values](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/argocd-app-of-apps-helm-values/): Use the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern with Helm value overrides per environment, enabling templated Application manifests and DRY multi-environment configurations.
- [Fix Helm Upgrade Failed and Rollback](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-upgrade-failed-troubleshooting/): Debug failed Helm releases stuck in pending-upgrade or failed state. Covers atomic upgrades, manual rollback, secret storage cleanup, and history limits.
- [Helm Chart Development from Scratch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-chart-development-guide/): Build production-ready Helm charts with templates, values, helpers, hooks, tests, and CI validation. Complete guide from chart create to publishing.
- [Helm Chart Testing and CI/CD Pipeline Integration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-chart-testing-ci/): Test Helm charts automatically with ct (chart-testing), helm unittest, and GitHub Actions. Validate templates, lint values, and run integration tests before release.
- [Helm Hooks for Database Migrations and Lifecycle Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-hooks-lifecycle/): Use Helm hooks to run database migrations, backups, and validation jobs during install, upgrade, and rollback. Control execution order with hook weights and deletion policies.
- ... and 29 more helm recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Networking (74 recipes)

- [Cilium Service Mesh: eBPF-Powered Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/cilium-service-mesh-kubernetes/): Deploy Cilium service mesh on Kubernetes with eBPF. Sidecar-free mTLS, L7 traffic management, network policies, Hubble observability, and Gateway API support.
- [Configure GPUDirect RDMA with the NVIDIA GPU Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/configure-gpudirect-rdma-gpu-operator/): Set up GPUDirect RDMA on Kubernetes using the NVIDIA GPU Operator with either DMA-BUF or legacy nvidia-peermem, including Network Operator integration.
- [Configure Knative Ingress Networking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/knative-ingress-networking/): Set up Knative Serving ingress with Kourier, Istio, or Contour. Custom domains, TLS, path routing, and external visibility.
- [Configure SR-IOV agent-config.yaml with Device by Path](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/agent-config-device-by-path/): Use agent-config.yaml to select network devices by PCI path for SR-IOV VF creation, ensuring deterministic NIC targeting across OpenShift nodes.
- [Configure SriovNetwork with NVIDIA nv-ipam](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/configure-sriovnetwork-nv-ipam/): Create a SriovNetwork resource that auto-generates a Multus NetworkAttachmentDefinition using nv-ipam for high-performance SR-IOV secondary interfaces.
- ... and 69 more networking recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Observability (35 recipes)

- [Alertmanager Routing, Grouping, and Silences](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/kubernetes-alertmanager-routing-silences/): Configure Alertmanager routing trees, receiver integrations, inhibition rules, silences, and alert grouping for production Kubernetes monitoring stacks.
- [EFK Stack: Kubernetes Centralized Logging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/kubernetes-logging-elk-stack/): Deploy EFK stack for Kubernetes centralized logging. Elasticsearch, Fluentd, Kibana setup, log collection, parsing, and retention policies.
- [Enable User Workload Monitoring OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/openshift-user-workload-monitoring/): Enable user workload monitoring on OpenShift. Deploy ServiceMonitor, PodMonitor, alerting rules, and Grafana dashboards.
- [GPU Tenant SLO Observability](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/gpu-tenant-slo-observability/): Define and monitor GPU tenant SLOs for queue time, inference latency, GPU utilization, and job completion rate with Prometheus alerting.
- [Grafana Dashboard 6417: Kubernetes Pod Monitoring](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/observability/grafana-kubernetes-dashboards/): Set up Grafana dashboard 6417 for Kubernetes pod monitoring. Import, customize panels, PromQL queries, and cluster-wide resource visualization.
- ... and 30 more observability recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Security (83 recipes)

- [AI Security Platforms on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/ai-security-platform-kubernetes/): Secure AI workloads on Kubernetes. Model supply chain security, prompt injection defense, LLM output filtering, AI RBAC, GPU isolation, and inference API protection.
- [Add Custom CA Certificates in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/custom-ca-kubernetes/): Configure custom Certificate Authority trust in vanilla Kubernetes using ConfigMap mounts, node-level trust stores, and containerd registry configuration.
- [Add Custom CA Certificates in OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/custom-ca-openshift/): Configure custom Certificate Authority trust across an OpenShift cluster using proxy config, image config, and automatic CA bundle injection into pods.
- [Add Custom CA in OpenShift and Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/custom-ca-openshift-kubernetes/): Configure custom Certificate Authority trust in both OpenShift and vanilla Kubernetes for private registries, internal services, and corporate PKI.
- [Automated Secret Rotation on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/security/kubernetes-enterprise-secret-rotation/): Implement zero-downtime secret rotation with External Secrets Operator, HashiCorp Vault dynamic secrets, and rolling restarts for enterprise compliance.
- ... and 78 more security recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Storage (37 recipes)

- [Backup and Restore OpenClaw State on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/openclaw-backup-restore/): Implement backup and disaster recovery for OpenClaw on Kubernetes with VolumeSnapshots, CronJobs to S3, and restore procedures for messaging sessions.
- [CNPG Disaster Recovery and Replication](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/cnpg-disaster-recovery/): Set up cross-region PostgreSQL disaster recovery with CloudNativePG using replica clusters, WAL shipping, and automated failover.
- [Configure S3 Storage Permissions for ML Models](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/s3-model-storage-permissions/): Set up S3 bucket ACLs, IAM roles, and PVC permissions so Kubernetes inference pods can securely read large ML model weights from object storage.
- [Distributed fio Storage Benchmarking on Kubernetes and OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/distributed-fio-kubernetes-openshift/): Run distributed fio benchmarks on Kubernetes and OpenShift to test storage performance at scale. Covers fio-distributed with k8s Jobs, Red Hat dbench, and CSI throughput validation.
- [Enable GPUDirect Storage on OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/storage/enable-gpudirect-storage-openshift/): Configure GPUDirect Storage (GDS) with the NVIDIA GPU Operator on OpenShift, including the Open Kernel Module requirement and nvidia-fs verification.
- ... and 32 more storage recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)

## Troubleshooting (80 recipes)

- [Check Bonding and Interface Status for SR-IOV](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/check-bonding-and-interface-status/): Inspect bond membership, interface state, and link aggregation to confirm which NICs can be correctly targeted by SR-IOV network policies on Kubernetes.
- [CrashLoopBackOff: How to Fix in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/debug-crashloopbackoff/): Fix CrashLoopBackOff in Kubernetes pods. Learn why pods crash loop, systematic debugging with kubectl logs and describe, and solutions for common causes.
- [Debug CRI-O Container Runtime Errors](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/crio-container-runtime-errors/): Troubleshoot CRI-O issues on OpenShift nodes. Fix image pull failures, container start errors, storage driver problems, and CNI networking plugin failures.
- [Debug DNS Resolution Failures in Pods](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/dns-resolution-failure-pods/): Troubleshoot pods unable to resolve DNS names. Check CoreDNS health, ndots configuration, search domains, and NetworkPolicies blocking UDP port 53 DNS traffic.
- [Debug Kubernetes Pods: Complete Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-debug-pods/): troubleshooting
- ... and 75 more troubleshooting recipes in [full index](https://kubernetes.recipes/llms-full.txt)
